perturbation.original_text: 4. Service Fee Service Recipient shall pay Service Provider the [service fees]/ [commissions and service fees] ([collectively,] "Service Fee") (inclusive of all applicable taxes) in accordance with Schedule 1 for the Services provided by Service Provider. [To facilitate the operation of Service Recipient, in the event that Service Recipient incurs losses, Service Provider may agree to waive the obligations of Service Recipient to pay Service Fee to Service Provider.]
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Service Fee Service Recipient shall pay Service Provider the [service fees]/ [commissions and service fees] ([collectively,] "Service Fee") (inclusive of all applicable taxes) in accordance with Schedule 1 for the Services provided by Service Provider. To facilitate the operation of Service Recipient, in the event that Service Recipient incurs losses, Service Provider will automatically waive the obligations of Service Recipient to pay Service Fee to Service Provider.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'may agree to waive' to 'will automatically waive' creates a mandatory condition for waiving service fees based on losses. This alters the original intent and could conflict with regulations governing revenue recognition and financial reporting.
perturbation.contradicted_law: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) and potentially Chinese Accounting Standards (CAS)
perturbation.law_citation: IAS 18 Revenue/IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers
perturbation.law_explanation: IAS 18/IFRS 15 specifies the conditions under which revenue can be recognized.  An 'automatic' waiver irrespective of specific conditions (e.g., documented loss verification, pre-agreed thresholds) conflicts with the principle of revenue recognition based on the probability of economic benefits flowing to the entity and the ability to reliably measure revenue.  The change introduces uncertainty on how revenue is recognized, potentially violating IFRS/CAS principles.
perturbation.justification: The original contract allows the Service Provider to 'agree to waive' service fees if the Service Recipient incurs losses, retaining discretion. The changed contract states the Service Provider 'will automatically waive' service fees, creating a mandatory waiver. The Law Explanation states that this 'automatic' waiver conflicts with revenue recognition principles under IAS 18/IFRS 15, which require consideration of the probability of economic benefits flowing to the entity. The changed text eliminates the consideration and agreement elements from the original contract, potentially leading to non-compliance with accounting standards, as per the Law Explanation. The existence of "IAS" as a scraped law snippet indicates that the IAS 18/IFRS 15 standards are potentially relevant.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Intellectual Property All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider. Service Recipient does not obtain any intellectual property rights in respect of the Services by entering into this Agreement. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Intellectual Property All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider. Service Recipient does not obtain any intellectual property rights in respect of the Services by entering into this Agreement. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be jointly owned by Service Provider and Service Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider' to 'jointly owned by Service Provider and Service Recipient' creates a direct conflict regarding intellectual property ownership. This changes the fundamental nature of the agreement regarding IP and could create enforceability problems under Chinese IP law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 13
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 13 of the Copyright Law of the PRC describes copyright ownership.  While joint ownership is permissible, simply stating 'jointly owned' without specifying the terms of that ownership (e.g., shares, decision-making processes) creates ambiguity and conflicts with the requirement for clarity in defining ownership rights under Chinese law. The original text established clear ownership, the modified text introduces ambiguity.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Procedures of Customs of the People's Republic of China for the Levying of Import Tax on Travellers' Luggage Articles and Personal Mailed Parcels May 31, 2010 --------------------- Monday >>>Welcome visitor, you're not logged in. Login Subscribe Now! Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Procedures of Customs of the People's Republic of China for the Levying of Import Tax on Travellers' Luggage Articles and Personal Mailed Parcels [Revised] 中华人民共和国海关关于入境旅客行李物品和个人邮递物品征收进口税办法 [已被修订] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Agencies , General Administration of Customs Document Number： Order No.18 of the General Administration of Customs Date issued： 03-05-1991 Effective date: 04-01-1991 Level of Authority: Departmental Rules Area of Law: Taxation Revised by： Provisions of the People's Republic of China on the Levy of Import Duties on Personal Luggage of Passengers and Personal Postal Parcels Arriving in China (Issued on 07-01-1994 Effective on 07-01-1994) Order of the General Administration of Customs (No. 18) The Procedures of the Customs of the People's Republic of China for the Levying of Import Tax on Travellers' Luggage Articles and Personal Mailed Parcels, as discussed and adopted by the Customs Tariff Commission of the State Council, are hereby issued and shall come into force as of April 1, 1991. The Rules of the Customs of the People's Republic of China Governing the Levying of Import Duties on the Articles in Passengers' Baggage and Personal Postal Parcels issued and implemented by the former Ministry of Foreign Trade on August 1, 1978 shall be abolished at the same time. Director General: Dai Jie March 5, 1991 PROCEDURES OF CUSTOMS OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA FOR THE LEVYING OF IMPORT TAX ON TRAVELLERS' LUGGAGE ARTICLES AND PERSONAL MAILED PARCELS (Promulgated by Decree No. 18 of the Customs General Administration of the People's Republic of China on March 5, 1991) 中华人民共和国海关总署令 （第18号） 《 中华人民共和国海关关于入境旅客行李物品和个人邮递物品征收进口税办法 》已经国务院关税税则委员会讨论通过，现予发布，自一九九一年四月一日起实行。一九七八年八月一日由原对外贸易部公布施行的《中华人民共和国关于 入境旅客行李物品和个人邮递物品征收进口税办法 》同时予以废止。 署长 戴杰 一九九一年三月五日 中华人民共和国海关关于入境旅客行李物品和个人邮递物品征收进口税办法 （1991年3月5日） Article 1 With a view to satisfying the rational need of imports for personal use and simplifying taxation procedures, the procedures hereof are formulated in accordance with " The Customs Law " and " Regulations on Import and Export Duties ". 第一条 为了照顾个人进口自用物品的合理需要，简化计税手续，根据《 海关法 》和《进出口关税条例》的有关规定，特制定本办法。 Article 2 Except otherwise stipulated, and import tax shall be levied on taxable travellers' luggage articles and personal mailed parcels and other articles for personal use (hereinafter referred to as personal articles) that are allowed to enter the country by the customs in accordance with "Import Tax Rates for Travellers' Luggage Articles and "Personal Mailed Parcels" (hereinafter referred to as "Rates"). The import tax referred to in the procedures hereof includes customs duties and consolidated industrial and commercial tax. The "Rates" is a part of the procedures hereof. Any re-adjustment of the "Rates" will be proposed by the Customs General Administration, and examined and endorsed by the Customs Duty Legislative Committee of the State Council before being promulgated and becoming effective. 第二条 准许应税进口的旅客行李物品、个人邮递物品以及其他个人自用物品（以下简称应税个人自用物品），除另有规定的以外，均由海关按照《旅客行李物品和个人邮递物品进口税税率表》征收进口税。 本办法所称的进口税，包括关税和工商统一税。 《旅客行李物品和个人邮递物品进口税税率表》（以下简称《税率表》）是本办法的组成部分。《税率表》中税率的调整，由海关总署提出，报经国务院关税税则委员会审定后，对外公布实施。 Article 3 Payers of import taxes are: entering travellers carrying taxable personal articles, staff of transport vehicles, recipients of imported mailed parcels and recipients of imported taxable articles through other means. Tax-payers may go through taxation procedures by himself of herself or by entrusting an agent to handle the matter on his or her behalf. The agents entrusted shall abide by the provisions bind
perturbation.justification: The original contract clearly assigns all intellectual property rights to the Service Provider. The changed text modifies this to 'jointly owned by Service Provider and Service Recipient'. The Law Explanation states this creates ambiguity and conflicts with the requirement for clarity in defining ownership rights under Chinese law. The change from exclusive ownership to joint ownership directly contradicts the original intent of sole ownership and introduces potential disputes due to the lack of specific terms governing the joint ownership.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law The Parties shall seek to resolve all the disputes arising from or in connection with this Agreement through friendly consultation. In the event that any dispute cannot be resolved through such consultation, such dispute shall be submitted to the Beijing Arbitration Commission ("BAC") for arbitration pursuant to the then effective arbitration procedures and rules of BAC. The arbitral award shall be final and binding upon the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law The Parties shall seek to resolve all the disputes arising from or in connection with this Agreement through friendly consultation. In the event that any dispute cannot be resolved through such consultation, such dispute shall be submitted to any arbitration center the Service Recipient chooses for arbitration pursuant to the then effective arbitration procedures and rules of that center. The arbitral award shall not be final or binding upon the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the designated arbitration commission from 'Beijing Arbitration Commission (BAC)' to 'any arbitration center the Service Recipient chooses' and removing the finality of the arbitral award creates an unenforceable clause, as it makes dispute resolution unilaterally controlled by one party and undermines the binding nature of arbitration agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 16
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 16 of the Arbitration Law of the PRC stipulates the requirements for a valid arbitration agreement, including a clear indication of the matters for arbitration and the chosen arbitration commission.  Allowing the Service Recipient to choose *any* arbitration center at the time of the dispute, rather than specifying it in the agreement, and stating that the award is not final, contradicts this requirement and effectively nullifies the arbitration clause, making it unenforceable under Chinese law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Circular of the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation and the Ministry of Communications on Issues Pertaining to the Establishment of Foreign-Funded Shipping Companies in China May 31, 2010 --------------------- Monday >>>Welcome visitor, you're not logged in. Login Subscribe Now! Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Circular of the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation and the Ministry of Communications on Issues Pertaining to the Establishment of Foreign-Funded Shipping Companies in China [Effective] 对外贸易经济合作部、交通部关于外国船公司在华设立独资船务公司有关问题的通知 [现行有效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Ministries , Ministry of Foreign Trade & Economic Cooperation (incl. former Ministry of Foreign Economy & Trade) , Ministry of Communications (dissolved) Date issued： 12-22-1995 Effective date: 12-22-1995 Level of Authority: Departmental Regulatory Documents Area of Law: Companies , Traffic and Transport CIRCULAR OF THE MINISTRY OF FOREIGN TRADE AND ECONOMIC COOPERATION AND THE MINISTRY OF COMMUNICATIONS ON ISSUES PERTAINING TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF FOREIGN FUNDED SHIPPING COMPANIES IN CHINA (December 22, 1995) With the gradual relaxing of control over the domestic shipping market, China has approved in succession some foreign shipping companies to establish shipping lines (hereinafter referred to as the solely foreign- owned shipping lines) in China. The following Circular is hereby issued in order to further the examination of and approval for establishing such lines: 对外贸易经济合作部、交通部关于外国船公司在华设立独资船务公司有关问题的通知 （［１９９５］外经贸资发第７８６号） 各省、自治区、直辖市及计划单列市外经贸（厅、局），深圳市引进外资办公室，各省、自治区、直辖市交通厅（委、办）： 随着我国航运市场的逐步放开，我国陆续批准了一些外国航运公司在华设立船务公司（以下简称独资船务公司）。为进一步做好设立此类公司的审批管理工作，现将有关问题通知如下： 1. The establishment of the solely foreign-owned shipping lines in China by foreign shipping companies must be examined and approved according to the commitment made by China on the reciprocal basis under the maritime transport agreement or memoranda of talks on maritime transport signed between the governments of China and the country concerned. 一、 审批外国航运公司在华设立独资船务公司必须依据两国政府签定的海运协定或海运会谈备忘录中我方按照对等原则所承诺的内容进行。 2. An applicant must possess the following qualifications: 二、 申请者须具备下列资格： (1) operating the shipping business for more than 15 years and the shipping representative office for three whole years at the cities where the solely foreign-owned shipping lines are to be established; （一）具有１５年以上的航运资历，在拟设独资船务公司的城市设航运代表处满３年； (2) its liners reach the ports of the cities where the solely foreign-owned shipping lines are to be established at least once per month. （二）其班轮船舶至少每月挂靠拟设独资船务公司所在城市港口一次。 3. The business scope of the solely foreign-owned shipping lines is: to provide cargo contracted to vessels owned and operated by their parent companies, to issue bills of landing on behalf of the parent companies, to settle freight and to conclude service contracts. ．．．．．． 三、 独资船务公司的经营范围是：为其母公司自己拥有和经营的船舶提供揽货、签发母公司提单、结算运费和签定服务合同业务。 ．．．．．． Dear visitor,you are attempting to view a subscription-based section of lawinfochina.com. perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the requirement for a clear indication of the chosen arbitration commission, as outlined in the Law Explanation. The original text specified the 'Beijing Arbitration Commission (BAC)', while the changed text allows the Service Recipient to choose 'any arbitration center'. This undermines the enforceability of the arbitration clause under PRC law, as it makes the choice of arbitration body unilateral and undefined within the agreement itself. Furthermore, the removal of the finality of the arbitral award directly contradicts the binding nature typically associated with arbitration agreements. Neither law snippet directly addresses arbitration agreements, but the absence does not negate the contradiction arising from the principles of PRC law and the details in the Perturbation and Law Explanations.
